title: Six as-u64 casts in db.rs violate DESIGN-sqlite-amount-cast; scan_tip worst case
description:
The same anti-pattern fixed in payment.rs (nie-91j.1) is repeated six times in db.rs. Project convention: use u64::try_from(i64_value) with a descriptive Err, never as u64.

Instances (all convert i64 DB values to u64 without checking for negative):

1. scan_tip() line 720: Ok(h as u64) — MOST DANGEROUS. If the DB scan_state row has a negative tip_height (e.g. from corruption or direct write), scan_tip() returns a value near u64::MAX. check_lag then sees local_tip >> chain_tip, computes saturating lag = 0, and the sync guard silently passes — allowing a spend with a wildly wrong anchor height. The spend would fail at proof time, but the guard is bypassed.

2. unspent_notes() lines 515,517: r.value_zatoshi as u64, r.block_height as u64 — corrupt note rows produce wrong amounts and heights silently.

3. all_sessions() line 444: r.amount_zatoshi as u64 — corrupt session rows show wrong amounts in /payments.

4. recent_txs() line 700: r.amount_zatoshi as u64 — same for tx history.

5-6. balance() lines 786-787: row.confirmed.unwrap_or(0) as u64, row.pending.unwrap_or(0) as u64 — SUM returns i64; a negative sum (corrupt data) produces a near-MAX balance display.

Fix: replace each as u64 with u64::try_from(...).map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("..."))?, matching the pattern in upsert_session (lines 395-400) and payment.rs.
